title: How the Wise Man Taught His Son: texts alpha, beta, and gamma
summary: CME00042 is the General Corpus copy of a University of Michigan electronic text derived from Rudolf Fischer's 1889 How the Wyse Man Taught Hys Sone: In drei Texten. The electronic selection contains Fischer's two title pages and printed pp. 27-49: alpha, based on Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38 and collated with Harley MS 5396; beta, based on Lambeth Palace Library MS 853 and collated with Balliol College MS 354; and gamma, based chiefly on Harley MS 2399 but supplemented through Frederick Furnivall's 1869 transcription of Bodleian MS Ashmole 61. Fischer worked from Hermann Varnhagen's transcripts for the first five witnesses rather than directly from those codices. Complete provider-exposed image sequences for Balliol MS 354 and Ashmole MS 61 have been cached privately, and each cache maps the poem's leaves inside the whole manuscript. No verified public complete facsimile was found for Ff.2.38, Harley 5396, Lambeth 853, or Harley 2399; request routes are recorded without converting those dated search results into claims that the manuscripts have never been digitized.
review_notes: Fischer's alpha, beta, and gamma are editorial groupings, not three independent works and not three extant codices.

Working transcripts are typed manuscript_witness because schema version 1.0.0 has no intermediary-transcript entity type; their labels, dates, survival status, and relation chains distinguish them from medieval witnesses.

Complete facsimile coverage is always relative to the provider's exposed IIIF inventory. The Balliol object lacks some physical exteriors and edges even though all 528 exposed canvases were cached; the Ashmole object contains all 358 exposed canvases.
A private, gitignored cache demonstrates download status but does not grant redistribution or publication rights.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00042

notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID are CME00042. TEMPHEAD records a 2016-08-23 review, not the initial encoding date.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item CME00042
entities.description: A manually keyed and proofread electronic selection from Fischer's 1889 edition, encoded with basic TEI semantics and 436 apparatus notes.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Fischer's 1889 edition
entities.reproduction_format: Complete downloadable PDF from a Google-digitized library copy

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38
entities.description: Fischer's C and base witness for alpha. Fischer gives fols. 45r-46r; the modern foliation places the poem at fols. 53r-54r.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 5396
entities.description: Fischer's H2, collated for alpha. The poem is at fols. 297r-300v within a poetry unit dated about 1456.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 853
entities.description: Fischer's L and base witness for beta. The poem occupies manuscript pp. 186-192 in the modern METS locator; Fischer gives pp. 186-193.

holding.notes: The catalog homepage is supplied because an auditable exact current public item page could not be retrieved.

entities.label: Balliol College MS 354
entities.description: Fischer's B, collated for beta. The poem begins on image page 335 and ends on page 337, corresponding to fols. 157r-158r; Stans puer ad mensam begins on page 338/f. 158v.

notes: Associated with the London grocer Richard Hill.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2399
entities.description: Fischer's incomplete H1 and principal base for gamma. The poem occupies fols. 61r-63v in a Bodmin/Cornwall production unit, perhaps written by John Arcuarius.

date_statements.label: manuscript part
date_statements.value: second half of the fifteenth century

notes: The relevant production unit is associated with Bodmin, Cornwall.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Ashmole 61
entities.description: Fischer's A branch, used indirectly through Furnivall's transcription. The poem is item 3 at fol. 6r-v; it omits stanzas 6-11, the first half of stanza 13, and stanza 14 in the catalog's reckoning.

notes: Made in England, perhaps Leicester; copied by the scribe who signs as Rate or Rathe.
No significant decoration or rubrication; yellow wash is used selectively. Rebound in 1986.

entities.label: Hermann Varnhagen's transcript of CUL MS Ff.2.38

access.access_method: No official public complete digital facsimile was verified in current catalog and discovery searches; a 1979 printed facsimile exists separately.

Use Cambridge's ordering-images route. Ask first for a digital reproduction of the complete physical manuscript—covers/boards, spine when photographable, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every leaf recto and verso, and any separately imaged edges—and separately identify the poem at modern fols. 53r-54r. Request confirmation of omissions, resolution, color, delivery format, price, and publication/redistribution terms.
notes: The published minimum charge is £25. Supplied copies are for research/private use by default; reproduction or online publication requires written permission.

access.access_method: The exact current catalog record exposes no Digitised Content link; this is dated search evidence, not proof that no internal or later digitization exists.

Use the catalog's Reference Services/contact route to ask whether a complete digital reproduction can be supplied. Define the requested physical coverage (boards/covers, spine if photographable, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every leaf recto/verso, edges if separately imaged), then separately identify the poem at fols. 297r-300v and ask about price, format, omissions, and publication rights.
The exact current catalog record exposes no Digitised Content link; this is dated search evidence, not proof that no internal or later digitization exists.

Use the catalog's Reference Services/contact route to ask whether a complete digital reproduction can be supplied. Define the requested physical coverage (boards/covers, spine if photographable, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every leaf recto/verso, edges if separately imaged), then separately identify the poem at fols. 61r-63v and ask about price, format, omissions, and publication rights.

No auditable official complete public facsimile was verified. The current archive catalog endpoint was inaccessible during review and the LUNA interface presented an access-verification barrier; neither was bypassed.

Use the online imaging-services order form. Ask first for a digital reproduction of the complete physical manuscript—covers/boards, spine when photographable, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every page/leaf, and any separately imaged edges—and separately identify the poem at pp. 186-192. Request confirmation of omissions, resolution, color, delivery format, total price, and publication/redistribution terms.
notes: The posted price list gives £14.40 per high-resolution image; standard PDFs are £8.40 for the first image and £1.20 for each additional image, with a 40-image order limit. Permission to reproduce is separate and supplied rights are nontransferable.

rights.component: U-M CME00042 text and presentation as offered
rights.basis: The U-M item identifies the material as public domain for educational and research use and links the No Copyright - United States statement.
notes: This statement does not grant rights in modern manuscript photography or unrelated modern editions.

rights.component: Fischer's 1889 editorial text, arrangement, introduction, apparatus, and notes
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1889 and the U-M derived item identifies its source material as public domain in the United States.
The underlying text is a fifteenth-century work; this assessment is separate from any modern edition, encoding, photograph, or contractual access condition.

rights.component: Internet Archive/Google scan files and digital reproduction
rights.basis: The underlying edition is public domain and the files are openly downloadable, but the item provides no express reusable-file license; the Google usage page asks for noncommercial personal use, no automated queries, and retention of the watermark.
notes: Public download access is not treated as permission to republish the scan derivative.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files and digital reproduction
rights.basis: The underlying edition is public domain and the PDF is openly downloadable, but current item metadata supplies no explicit reusable-file license for the derivative.

rights.component: Internet Archive/Google scan files and digital reproduction
rights.basis: The underlying edition is public domain and the PDF is openly downloadable, but the item provides no express reusable-file license for the derivative.

rights.component: Balliol MS 354 photography and Digital Bodleian delivery
rights.basis: The manifest attributes the photographs © The Master and Fellows of Balliol College, Oxford, marks all rights reserved, and requires permission to reproduce images including online.
notes: Balliol permits downloading or printing one copy for private study or research; online or other publication requires written permission. The private cache is not redistributable on that basis.

rights.component: Ashmole MS 61 photography and Digital Bodleian delivery
rights.basis: Digital Bodleian attributes the item under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 and identifies Bodleian Libraries as photographer/rightsholder.
notes: Commercial reuse, including profit-oriented publication, falls outside this license unless separately permitted.

rights.component: Selected Ashmole MS 61 slide photography and Digital Bodleian delivery
rights.basis: The separate Digital Bodleian slide object is offered under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0.

rights.component: METS digital edition, editorial text, notes, and downloadable PDF
rights.basis: METS states that its digital editions are copyrighted and for individual use only, and that reprinting requires permission. Its CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 notice applies specifically to downloadable XML, not automatically to the PDF or complete edition.
notes: The locally cached PDF is for private research and must not be committed or redistributed without permission.

rights.component: Any newly supplied Cambridge manuscript photography
rights.basis: Cambridge's supply terms limit normal orders to research/private use and require written permission for reproduction, including online publication.

rights.component: Any newly supplied Lambeth manuscript photography
rights.basis: Lambeth's imaging page separates supply from permission to reproduce and states that supplied rights are nontransferable.

rights.component: Any newly supplied British Library manuscript photography for Harley 2399 or Harley 5396
rights.basis: No item-specific reproduction license was verified. Any quotation, publication, or repository redistribution must follow the terms supplied with an order or written response.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Six manuscript witnesses accessed through five Varnhagen transcripts and one Furnivall print transcription
editorial_practices.representation: Three edited version-groups with paired-witness variants in footnotes
editorial_practices.description: Fischer prints alpha from C with H2 variants, beta from L with B variants, and gamma chiefly from incomplete H1 with A material supplied through Furnivall. He corrects obvious errors from the paired witness, sometimes regularizes meter with paired support, and records variants extensively.
editorial_practices.scope: Fischer's texts and apparatus on pp. 27-49

editorial_practices.description: Fischer explicitly says manuscript abbreviations are expanded but not distinguished by italics; therefore italic type in the CME-derived text should not automatically be interpreted as a facsimile of scribal abbreviation marks.
Fischer states that i/j and u/v are normalized. This practice is modeled under the closest schema enum; it is a letter-form normalization, not principally a capitalization claim.
editorial_practices.scope: All three edited texts and apparatus

editorial_practices.representation: Two title pages plus printed pp. 27-49
editorial_practices.description: The electronic item omits Fischer's introduction pp. 1-26 and notes/index pp. 50-64 while retaining the three edited texts and their apparatus.

evidence.citation: General Corpus source XML for CME00042
evidence.summary: Primary repository evidence for identifiers, title pages, encoded text, printed pagination, apparatus, and revision note.

evidence.citation: Structural inspection and element count of CME00042.xml at CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c
evidence.locator: BODY, three DIV2 version containers, title-page DIV1 elements, and PB/N milestone sequence
evidence.summary: Confirmed three version containers, 63 LG stanzas, 504 base L elements, 436 NOTE elements, 100 line-number milestones, 25 PB elements, and the discontinuous title-page/pp. 27-49 source scope.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00042
evidence.summary: Identifies Fischer's 1889 source, lists alpha, beta, and gamma, and supplies the U-M public-domain rights statement.

evidence.citation: Rudolf Fischer, ed., How the Wyse Man Taught Hys Sone: In drei Texten (Erlangen and Leipzig, 1889)
evidence.locator: title pages; introduction pp. 1-26; edited texts pp. 27-49; notes and index pp. 50-64
evidence.summary: The complete primary source edition for bibliography, source scope, texts, apparatus, and Fischer's witness statements.

evidence.citation: Fischer 1889, introduction and editorial statement
evidence.locator: printed pp. 1-26, especially the witness descriptions and editorial method immediately preceding p. 27
evidence.summary: States that Varnhagen supplied transcripts of C, H2, L, B, and H1; that Furnivall's 1869 text supplied A; that alpha uses C/H2, beta L/B, and gamma H1/A; and that Fischer corrects obvious errors, sometimes normalizes meter, expands abbreviations without italics, modernizes i/j and u/v, and records paired variants.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata and scan-data for HowTheWyseManTaught
evidence.summary: Supports exact file URLs, scan provenance, paging, and the absence of an explicit reusable-file license; the Google usage notice supplies noncommercial personal-use conditions.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38; Frances McSparran and P. R. Robinson, facsimile (1979)
evidence.summary: Supplies the current poem locator fols. 53r-54r and bibliographic evidence for the complete 1979 printed facsimile, allowing Fischer's older fols. 45r-46r to be retained as a legacy locator.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, ordering images
evidence.summary: Provides the institutional route for ordering manuscript reproductions and current minimum-charge information.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, terms and conditions for image supply and reproduction
evidence.summary: Limits ordinary supplied images to private research/study and requires written permission for reproduction and publication.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 5396
evidence.locator: poetry unit ff. 271r-311v; poem ff. 297r-300v from Fischer/METS cross-reference
evidence.summary: Supplies the exact current record, poetry-unit date about 1456, holding, and absence of an exposed Digitised Content link at review time.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 2399
evidence.summary: Identifies the incomplete poem, second-half-fifteenth-century relevant part, Bodmin/Cornwall association, possible scribe John Arcuarius, and absence of an exposed Digitised Content link at review time.

evidence.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Early English Meals and Manners (1868), Lambeth MS 853 description
evidence.summary: Historical scholarly evidence for the conventional circa-1430 date and parchment support of Lambeth MS 853; the date remains attributed rather than inferred from another witness.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library, Imaging Services
evidence.summary: Provides the online order route, current image/PDF prices and order limits, and separate nontransferable permission-to-reproduce conditions.

evidence.citation: Dated search of Lambeth archive catalog and image-discovery interfaces for MS 853
evidence.summary: No auditable official complete public image object was verified on 2026-07-12; the archive endpoint was inaccessible and the LUNA interface presented an access-verification barrier. This supports only a dated no-public-copy-found status.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF manifest for Balliol College MS 354
evidence.summary: Exposes 528 canvases, manuscript metadata, and an all-rights-reserved Balliol attribution requiring permission for online reproduction.

evidence.citation: Local audit of the complete provider-exposed Balliol MS 354 IIIF image bundle
evidence.summary: Verified 528 of 528 exposed canvases, bundle integrity, and the poem's incipit and explicit within the full manuscript. The provider manifest runs from inside upper board to inside lower board but omits several physical exteriors and edges.

evidence.citation: Balliol College Archives and Manuscripts, image use terms
evidence.summary: Allows one downloaded or printed copy for private study/research and requires written permission to publish manuscript images, including on a website.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS Ashmole 61
evidence.summary: Supplies exact contents, incipit/explicit, DIMEV number, omissions, date, tentative region, support, extent, dimensions, scribe, decoration, binding, and a link to the complete Digital Bodleian object.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF manifest for MS Ashmole 61
evidence.summary: Exposes 358 canvases from upper board through former lower board, including fol. 6r-v at canvases 27-28, and applies CC BY-NC 4.0 to the digital images.

evidence.citation: Local audit of the complete provider-exposed Ashmole MS 61 IIIF image bundle and the separate selected-slide bundle
evidence.summary: Verified 358 of 358 exposed full-object canvases at the requested 1800-pixel width and visually located the poem. The eight-slide legacy object has no overlap with fol. 6r-v.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF manifest for selected 35mm slides of MS Ashmole 61
evidence.summary: A distinct eight-image partial object; it does not cover the poem, and must not be confused with the complete 358-canvas object.

evidence.citation: Frederick J. Furnivall, ed., Queene Elizabethes Achademy (London, 1869)
evidence.summary: Prints the Ashmole 61 version used by Fischer as his indirect source for A.

evidence.citation: Roman Dyboski, ed., Songs, Carols, and Other Miscellaneous Poems from the Balliol MS. 354 (London, 1907)
evidence.locator: item 59, printed pp. xlii-xliii; PDF pp. 59-60
evidence.summary: Confirms Balliol fols. 157r-158r and explains that Fischer's beta footnotes contain the Balliol readings rather than a continuous Balliol edition.

evidence.citation: Eve Salisbury, ed., The Trials and Joys of Marriage (Kalamazoo, 2002)
evidence.summary: Provides a modern readable edition, notes, bibliography, DIMEV/NIMEV identifiers, witness census, current locators, and genre context.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, Copyright and Citation
evidence.summary: States that digital editions are copyrighted and for individual use only, that reprinting requires permission, and that the CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 license applies to downloadable XML.

evidence.citation: Dated official-catalog and public-viewer searches for complete facsimiles of CUL Ff.2.38, Harley 5396, and Harley 2399
evidence.summary: No official public complete digital object was verified for these manuscripts on 2026-07-12. These are search-result claims with request alternatives, not assertions that digitization is impossible or permanently absent.

open_questions.question: Can Cambridge supply a complete color digital reproduction of CUL MS Ff.2.38, and under what private-use, repository-publication, and commercial-publication terms?
next_steps: Use the recorded Cambridge image-order route and request the whole physical manuscript before naming fols. 53r-54r as the work portion.

Ask for covers/boards, spine if photographable, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every leaf recto/verso, and any separately imaged edges; request an omissions list, resolution, color profile, file format, price, and delivery method.
Ask separately whether the delivered files or derived reference crops may be placed in a public Git repository or used in profit-oriented print-on-demand research.

open_questions.question: Can the British Library supply complete digital reproductions of Harley MS 5396 and Harley MS 2399, and what reuse terms would attach?
next_steps: Submit separate reference enquiries for both shelfmarks, defining complete physical coverage before identifying the work portions at Harley 5396 fols. 297r-300v and Harley 2399 fols. 61r-63v.
Ask whether existing photography already covers the whole objects; if not, request a quotation and an explicit list of omitted boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, leaves, or edges.
Ask for written repository and commercial-publication terms before caching or redistributing any supplied files.

open_questions.question: Can Lambeth Palace Library supply a complete digital reproduction of MS 853 despite the posted 40-image order limit, and what would the total cost and reuse terms be?
next_steps: Use the imaging-services form to ask whether a multi-order or custom whole-manuscript project is possible, defining complete physical coverage first and pp. 186-192 second.
Ask whether a pre-existing complete microfilm or digital surrogate can be supplied more economically than per-image photography.
Obtain separate written permission terms for public Git hosting, scholarly quotation, and commercial print-on-demand research before redistributing supplied images.

open_questions.question: Do Hermann Varnhagen's five working transcripts survive, and can their readings be checked against the codices?
next_steps: Search Varnhagen archival papers, Fischer correspondence, and Erlangen editorial archives for transcripts of Ff.2.38, Harley 5396, Lambeth 853, Balliol 354, and Harley 2399.
If found, catalog each transcript's shelfmark, completeness, date, and deviations from both codex and Fischer.

open_questions.question: What exact physical change accounts for Fischer's fols. 45r-46r and the modern fols. 53r-54r in CUL MS Ff.2.38?
next_steps: Consult the 1979 facsimile introduction and the current CUL catalog when accessible.
Record any flyleaf or inserted-leaf counting rule that explains the eight-folio offset rather than silently replacing the historical citation.

open_questions.question: How are the six manuscript witnesses and Fischer's alpha, beta, and gamma related beyond Fischer's unresolved grouping?
next_steps: Compare complete diplomatic transcriptions or facsimiles of all six witnesses rather than inferring a stemma from Fischer's edited groupings.
Review recent textual scholarship for a defensible relationship model and retain uncertainty where dependence or contamination cannot be established.
